symptoms.symptom_name: Acute symmetrical myalgia with low-grade fever and malaise

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical myalgia/arthralgia with malaise fits DILE.
Febrile myalgia is non-specific and can be infective.
Hydralazine is a classic cause of drug-induced lupus with a typical weeks-to-months latency.
No obvious source lowers, but does not exclude, infection given only history is available.

symptoms.symptom_name: No sepsis danger signs (clear mentation, no rigors/dyspnoea)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of danger signs lowers immediate sepsis probability but in-person assessment still required.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Same-day clinical assessment with observations and septic screen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Exclude infection with full observations and examination — the key step telehealth cannot provide.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: FBC, CRP, blood cultures, renal function
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess for infection/inflammation and renal function before any NSAID.
Support the DILE diagnosis on outpatient follow-up.
